title: Fix podman pod unpause TODO
body:
Update the podman pod unpause to only show the paused
containers with autocomplete.
Fix a typo in the help command.
Update the unpause function to only attempt an unpause
on pasued pods instead of all the pods.
Update the tests accordingly.

Signed-off-by: Urvashi Mohnani <umohnani@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
podman pod unpause only shows paused pods when using autocomplete

```

comments.body:
LGTM
But tests are not happy.

I think when doing a `pod unpause`, I would expect it to only unpause pods that are in the `paused` state. If a pod has multiple containers and only one container is paused, the pod ends up going in `Degraded` state. Looks like we restrict users from blocking a single container in a pod. Pausing and unpausing at the pod level works fine. So I don't think this would be breaking any behavior. 

This seems a bit confusing if we attempt to pause a pod even when it is in running state. We are also fixing the autocomplete to show IDs/names of paused pods only so we should follow that and just try to unpause paused pods and return those IDs.

current main:
```
$ bin/podman pod create --name test
cc16eac7f8d4c173046cdd6fb86929818bb8b0c7e46ff13bc16c67d7e8257b3d
$ bin/podman run --pod test --name c1 -d alpine top
24b1b18cc382fe16087bc27cf6262959f8ce6114479d8f5c508d8337cc320285
$ bin/podman pause c1
24b1b18cc382fe16087bc27cf6262959f8ce6114479d8f5c508d8337cc320285
$ bin/podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                        COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS             PORTS       NAMES
ce3e4736170f  localhost/podman-pause:4.2.0-dev-1654699615              19 seconds ago  Up 10 seconds ago              cc16eac7f8d4-infra
24b1b18cc382  docker.io/library/alpine:latest              top         9 seconds ago   Paused                         c1
$ bin/podman pod unpause test
cc16eac7f8d4c173046cdd6fb86929818bb8b0c7e46ff13bc16c67d7e8257b3d
$ bin/podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                        COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS             PORTS       NAMES
ce3e4736170f  localhost/podman-pause:4.2.0-dev-1654699615              28 seconds ago  Up 19 seconds ago              cc16eac7f8d4-infra
24b1b18cc382  docker.io/library/alpine:latest              top         18 seconds ago  Up 19 seconds ago              c1
```

your PR:
```
$ bin/podman pod create --name test
b0262289f2da68fe4539c18a9bcee5ab34b8a963a7a1877d4ecc73c0e60c5e7c
$ bin/podman run --pod test --name c1 -d alpine top
5e923ed4c14505b0054f78bbd694c4d6838bbc46f16589f2157713d32778a3aa
$ bin/podman pause c1
5e923ed4c14505b0054f78bbd694c4d6838bbc46f16589f2157713d32778a3aa
$ bin/podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                        COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS            PORTS       NAMES
4f886a627a78  localhost/podman-pause:4.2.0-dev-1654699847              10 seconds ago  Up 7 seconds ago              b0262289f2da-infra
5e923ed4c145  docker.io/library/alpine:latest              top         6 seconds ago   Paused                        c1
$ bin/podman pod unpause test
$ bin/podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                        COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS             PORTS       NAMES
4f886a627a78  localhost/podman-pause:4.2.0-dev-1654699847              18 seconds ago  Up 14 seconds ago              b0262289f2da-infra
5e923ed4c145  docker.io/library/alpine:latest              top         13 seconds ago  Paused                         c1
```


This can clearly break user who rely on this behaviour. I see no reason why pod unpause should only work when all containers are paused.

@Luap99 thanks for pointing that out. I have fixed up the PR to only try to unpause paused or degraded pods (this is the state the pod is in when only some of the containers in the pod have been paused).
PTAL
